mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of declining rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side slack, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of industrial production contraction periods during post-shock recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters showing positive GDPC1 growth over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding high-capacity thresholds signals strong manufacturing demand and pricing power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when INDPRO exceeds the threshold.
